programmes.show_description: Italian baritone Andrè Schuen and pianist Daniel Heide give a recital at the Schubertiade Schwarzenberg in Austria, including Wagner's Wesendonck Lieder and Strauss's Four Songs.
Linton Stephens presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Al Ryan with concert recordings of distinctive settings of the church mass, today with music by Karl Jenkins, plus music from the recent Belfast International Chamber Music Festival.
2/5. Kate Molleson surveys the Third Symphony by Sibelius and the period it was composed - a work which led one critic to call Sibelius a Classical Master.
Katie Derham introduces live music from EMPOWER: Women Changing Music, and conductor Paavo Jarvi and pianist Alexandre Kantorow join us in the studio.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
The BBC Symphony Orchestra conducted by Daniele Rustioni performs Bernstein, Rachmaninov's Symphony No.3, and ANTHEM a collaboration with baritone Davóne Tines for USA 250.

programmes.show_description: Legendary broadcaster Gillian Reynolds shares her life and career in radio, spanning over 50 years. Her theme today - the power of the voice....
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Chilean tenor saxophonist Melissa Aldana is back with her second Flowers pick of the week.
Haydn's 'Sunrise' Quartet and Beethoven's 'Razumovsky' Op 59 no 1 Quartet performed by one of the world's most enduring quartets at the Barcelona String Quartet Biennale.
Linton Stephens presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Al Ryan with exclusive recordings of music by Striggio and others plus another in the Wednesday series of concerts from the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra to take us into spring.
Live from the Chapel of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, with music by Joanna Marsh.
3/5. Kate Molleson follows the creation of the Fourth Symphony by Sibelius amid much self-doubt - a work at least one biographer has termed as his Psychological Symphony.
Petroc welcomes Her Ensemble  and author Eric McElroy to In Tune.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
Kahchun Wong conducts the Hallé Orchestra and Choirs at Bridgewater Hall in Manchester, in a programme of celestial music by Gustav Holst, Unsuk Chin and Colin Matthews.
